blocks.content: Hester's had been on the corner of Fifth and Birch since before any of them were born. It smelled like it — fried things and sweet tea and a vanilla milkshake no other place in Millhaven had ever replicated.
Eli had already claimed the big corner booth, Coke in hand. Nobody commented on how early he'd arrived.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I already know what everyone's getting. Kit gets the double with no onion. Daisy gets the regular with extra pickles and a chocolate shake. Savannah gets the chicken and acts like she's not jealous of our burgers.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Nobody genuinely prefers the chicken at Hester's.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: You look at my burger every single time.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: I look at everything. I'm observant.
Kit slid in across from Daisy and picked up the sticky menu he didn't need.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You don't understand a lot of things.
The waitress came. Her name was Gloria and she had worked at Hester's forever and always called them hon without distinction.

blocks.speaker: Gloria
blocks.content: Last Saturday of summer, hons. What'll it be?
Eli ordered for the whole table without being asked. Gloria didn't write any of it down.
